Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting additional COVID-19 measures
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act respecting additional COVID-19 measures
Identification.RunningHead: An Act respecting additional COVID-19 measures
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF EMPLOYMENT, WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AND DISABILITY INCLUSION

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act respecting additional COVID-19 measuresâ.
Part 1 amends the Income Tax Act to revise the eligibility criteria for the Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy in order to support those employers hardest hit by the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19).

Part 2 enacts the Time Limits and Other Periods Act (COVID-19), which addresses the need for flexibility in relation to certain time limits and other periods that are established by or under Acts of Parliament and that are difficult or impossible to meet as a result of the exceptional circumstances produced by COVID-19. In particular, the enactment

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: suspends, for a maximum of six months, certain time limits in relation to proceedings before courts;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: temporarily enables ministers to suspend or extend time limits and to extend other periods in relation to specified Acts and regulations for a maximum of six months; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: provides for the transparent exercise of the powers it confers and for Parliamentary oversight over the exercise of those powers.

Part 3 amends the Income Tax Act to authorize the use by officials, or disclosure to Government of Canada officials, of taxpayer information solely for the purpose of a one-time payment to persons with disabilities for reasons related to COVID-19. It also amends the Childrenâs Special Allowances Act to authorize the disclosure of information for the purpose of that one-time payment.

Part 4 amends the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act to, among other things, enhance the administration and enforcement of the Act and allow a review of decisions made under the Act. It also provides that a worker is not eligible for an income support payment if they do not return to work when it is reasonable to do so or decline a reasonable job offer.

Heading.TitleText: An Act respecting additional COVID-19 measures

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: Time Limits and Other Periods Act (COVID-19)
An Act respecting the suspension or extension of time limits and the extension of other periods as part of the response to the coronavirus disease 2019

Text.XRefExternal: Time Limits and Other Periods Act (COVID-19)

TableOfProvisions.Text: Ministerial orders â Acts and regulations

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Disclosure of Information for the Purpose of a One-time Payment to Persons with Disabilities

Provision.Text: Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Subsection 87(2) of the Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (g.5):

Text.Ins: for the purposes of section 125.7, the new corporation is deemed to be the same corporation as, and a continuation of, each predecessor corporation unless it is reasonable to consider that one of the main purposes of the amalgamation is to cause the new corporation to qualify for the deemed overpayment under subsection 125.7(2);

Subsection.Text: The definition baseline remuneration in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: baseline remuneration, in respect of an eligible employee of an eligible entity, means the average weekly eligible remuneration, excluding any period of seven or more consecutive days for which the employee was not remunerated, paid to the eligible employee by the eligible entity during the period that

Paragraph.Text: begins on January 1, 2020 and ends on March 15, 2020; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible entity elects for all qualifying periods,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: begins on March 1, 2019 and ends on May 31, 2019, in respect of a qualifying period described in any of paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition qualifying period, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is prescribed, in respect of a qualifying period described in paragraph (d) of the definition qualifying period. (rÃ©munÃ©ration de base)

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition eligible entity in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or a trust, other than a corporation or a trust that is exempt from tax under this Part or is a public institution;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual other than a trust;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition qualifying entity in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Text.Ins: it meets either of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Text: it had, on March 15, 2020, a business number in respect of which it is registered with the Minister to make remittances required under section 153, or

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: it employed one or more individuals in Canada,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the payroll for its employees was administered by another person or partnership (referred to in this subparagraph as the âpayroll service providerâ), and

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the payroll service provider had a business number in respect of which it is registered with the Minister to make remittances required under section 153,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the payroll service provider used its business number to make the remittances referred to in subclause (A)(III) in respect of the employees of the eligible entity, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the Minister is satisfied that the conditions in clauses (A) and (B) are met. (entitÃ© admissible)

Section.Text: Sections 1 and 2 are deemed to have come into force on April 11, 2020, except that, in respect of the periods described in paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition qualifying period in subsection 125.7(1) of the Income Tax Act, paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition eligible entity in subsection 125.7(1) of that Act, as enacted by subsection 2(2), are to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation, other than a corporation that is exempt from tax under this Part or is a public institution;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual;

TitleText.XRefExternal: Time Limits and Other Periods Act (COVID-19)

Section.Text: The Time Limits and Other Periods Act (COVID-19), whose text is as follows and whose schedule is set out in the schedule to this Act, is enacted:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act respecting the suspension or extension of time limits and the extension of other periods as part of the response to the coronavirus disease 2019

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Time Limits and Other Periods Act (COVID-19).
In this Act, period includes the time during which a licence, permit or other authorization is valid.

If a time limit is suspended or extended or a period is extended under this Act, then, during the period that the suspension or extension is in effect, every reference in any Act of Parliament or its regulations to that time limit or period is to be read as a reference to the time limit or period as it is suspended or extended.

Subsection.Text: This Act does not apply in respect of the investigation of an offence or in respect of a proceeding respecting an offence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Non-application â Corrections and Conditional Release Act
Subsection.Text: This Act does not apply in respect of a time limit or other period that is established by or under the Corrections and Conditional Release Act.
The purpose of this Act is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to temporarily suspend certain time limits and to temporarily authorize, in a flexible manner, the suspension or extension of other time limits in order to prevent any exceptional circumstances that may be produced by coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) from making it difficult or impossible to meet those time limits; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to temporarily authorize, in a flexible manner, the extension of other periods in order to prevent any unfair or undesirable effects that may result from the expiry of those periods due to those exceptional circumstances.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, this Act is to be interpreted in a manner that provides certainty in relation to proceedings and that respects the rule of law and the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

The following time limits are, if established by or under an Act of Parliament, suspended for the period that starts on March 13, 2020 and that ends on September 13, 2020 or on any earlier day fixed by order of the Governor in Council made on the recommendation of the Minister of Justice:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any limitation or prescription period for commencing a proceeding before a court;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any time limit in relation to something that is to be done in a proceeding before a court; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any time limit within which an application for leave to commence a proceeding or to do something in relation to a proceeding is to be made to a court.

Subsection.Text: The court may, by order, vary the suspension of a time limit as long as the commencement date of the suspension remains the same and the duration of the suspension does not exceed six months.
The court may make orders respecting the effects of a failure to meet a suspended time limit, including orders that cancel or vary those effects.
The Governor in Council may, by order made on the recommendation of the Minister of Justice, lift a suspension in circumstances specified in the order.

Section.MarginalNote: Ministerial orders â Acts and regulations

Subsection.Text: The minister who is responsible for an Act of Parliament set out in column 1 of the schedule or a relevant portion of the Act may make an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspending or extending a time limit that is established by or under any provision of the Act that is set out in column 2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: extending any other period that is established by or under any provision of the Act that is set out in column 2;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a regulation is set out in column 2 in respect of the Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: suspending or extending a time limit that is established by or under that regulation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: extending any other period that is established by or under that regulation; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: extending a suspension or extension.

Subsection.Text: The minister who is responsible for a regulation set out in column 1 of the schedule or a relevant portion of the regulation may make an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspending or extending a time limit that is established by or under any provision of the regulation that is set out in column 2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: extending any other period that is established by or under any provision of the regulation that is set out in column 2; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: extending a suspension or extension.

Subsection.Text: An order under subsection (1) or (2) does not apply in respect of a time limit or other period that ends on or after December 31, 2020.

The total duration of a suspension or extension must not exceed six months. However, a suspension must not have the effect of allowing a time limit to continue after December 31, 2020 and an extended time limit or period must end on or before that day.

An order under subsection (1) or (2) may, if it so provides, have retroactive effect, but not before March 13, 2020, and it may also include provisions respecting the effects of a failure to meet the time limit or of the expiry of the period before the day on which the order was made, including provisions that cancel or vary those effects.
An order under subsection (1) or (2) may provide that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a suspension or extension does not apply in respect of any circumstance specified in the order without the consent of a person, court or body specified in the order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a suspension or extension applies in respect of any circumstance specified in the order unless a person, court or body specified in the order decides otherwise; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person, court or body specified in the order may vary the effects of the order in relation to any circumstance specified in the order.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister of Justice, make regulations restricting, or imposing conditions on, a power to make an order under subsection (1) or (2).

Section.Text: The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection 6(1), (2), (3) or (4) or 7(1) or (2).
A power conferred on the Governor in Council or a minister under this Act is not to be exercised after September 30, 2020.

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection 6(1) or (4) or 7(1) or (2), together with the reasons for making it, must be published, as soon as feasible after the day on which it is made but no later than five days after the day on which it is made, on a Government of Canada website for a period of at least six months.
An order referred to in subsection (1) must be published in Part I of the Canada Gazette within 14 days after the day on which it is made.

An order made under subsection 6(1) or (4) or 7(1) or (2) must be tabled in each House of Parliament within three days after the day on which it is made, unless a House is not sitting within those three days, in which case the order is to be tabled in that House at the earliest opportunity.
An order that is tabled in a House of Parliament must be referred to a committee of that House.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Disclosure of Information for the Purpose of a One-time Payment to Persons with Disabilities

Section.Text: Subsection 241(4) of the Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: use, or provide to an official of a department or agency of the Government of Canada, taxpayer information solely for a purpose relating to the administration or enforcement of a program to provide a one-time payment to persons with disabilities for reasons related to the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), to the extent that the information is relevant for the purpose;

Section.Text: Subsection 10(2) of the Childrenâs Special Allowances Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any information obtained by or on behalf of the Minister in the course of the administration or enforcement of this Act or the regulations or the carrying out of an agreement entered into under section 11 may be communicated

Paragraph.Text: to any person if it can reasonably be regarded as necessary for the purposes of the administration or enforcement of this Act, the Income Tax Act, the Canada Disability Savings Act or the Canada Education Savings Act or a program administered under an agreement entered into under section 12 of the Canada Education Savings Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to the Department of Employment and Social Development, to the extent that the communication is required for the administration of a program to provide a one-time payment to persons with disabilities for reasons related to the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19).

Section.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A worker may, in the form and manner established by the Minister, apply for an income support payment

Paragraph.Text: for any period of four weeks falling within the period beginning on March 15, 2020 and ending on July 4, 2020; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for any period of two weeks falling within the period beginning on July 5, 2020 and ending on October 3, 2020.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 6(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for reasons related to COVID-19 and whether as an employee or in self-employment,

Subparagraph.Text: in the case of the first application made under paragraph 5(1)(a), the worker ceases working for a period of at least 14 consecutive days within the period in respect of which they apply for the payment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of any other application made under paragraph 5(1)(a) or any application made under paragraph 5(1)(b), the worker ceases working for the period in respect of which they apply for the payment; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 6(1)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the worker does not receive, in respect of the period for which they have ceased working,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 6(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (2.1), a worker is eligible for an income support payment if
Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
A worker is not eligible for an income support payment if they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fail to return to work when it is reasonable to do so and the employer makes a request for their return;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fail to resume self-employment when it is reasonable to do so; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: decline a reasonable job offer when they are able to work.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8:

Subsection.Text: A person who is the subject of a decision of the Minister in respect of an application made by that person under section 5 may make a request, in the form and manner established by the Minister, to the Minister for a review of that decision at any time within 30 days after the day on which they are informed of the decision or any further time that the Minister may allow.
The Minister must review the decision if a request for its review is made under subsection (1). On completion of the review, the Minister must confirm, vary or rescind the decision.
The Minister must inform the person who made the request of the Ministerâs decision under subsection (2).
The Ministerâs confirmation, variation or rescission, as the case may be, is final and is not to be questioned or reviewed in any court.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 12:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may impose on a person who made an application for an income support payment under section 5, or on another person acting for that person, a penalty for each of the following acts or omissions if the Minister becomes aware of facts that in the Ministerâs opinion establish that the person or other person has

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to the application, made a representation that they knew was false or misleading;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: being required under this Act to provide information, provided information or made a representation that they knew was false or misleading;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly failed to declare to the Minister all or some of the personâs income for the period in respect of which they applied for the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: made an application or declaration under this Act that they knew was false or misleading because of the non-disclosure of facts;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly, in any manner, received an income support payment that they were not eligible to receive under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: participated in, assented to or acquiesced in an act or omission mentioned in paragraphs (a) to (e).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may set the amount of the penalty for each act or omission at not more than three times the amount of an income support payment for a week that falls within the period in respect of which the application was made.

Section.Text: A penalty must not be imposed under section 12.1 if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a prosecution for the act or omission has been initiated against the person who made an application for an income support payment under section 5 or another person who acted for that person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 36 months have passed since the day on which the act or omission occurred.

Section.Text: The Minister may rescind the imposition of a penalty under section 12.1, or reduce the penalty, on the presentation of new facts or on being satisfied that the penalty was imposed without knowledge of, or on the basis of a mistake as to, some material fact.
The Minister may issue a warning instead of setting the amount of a penalty for an act or omission under subsection 12.1.

Section.MarginalNote: Recovery as debt due to Her Majesty

Subsection.Text: A penalty imposed under section 12.1 constitutes a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered by the Minister.

The amount of any debt referred to in subsection (1) may be certified by the Minister and registration of the certificate in the Federal Court has the same effect as a judgment of that Court for the amount specified in the certificate and all related registration costs.

Section.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that a person is or is about to become liable to pay an amount to another person who is indebted to Her Majesty under subsection 12(2) or 12.5(1), the Minister may, by written notice, order the person to pay to the Receiver General on account of the other personâs liability all or part of the amount otherwise payable to the other person.
Subsection 13(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The running of a limitation or prescription period in respect of money owing under this Act is suspended

Paragraph.Text: during any period in which it is prohibited to commence or continue an action or other proceedings against the person to recover money owing under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: during any period in which any review of a decision establishing the liability in respect of money owing under this Act is pending.

Section.Text: Section 14 of the Act is replaced by the following:
No interest is payable on any amount owing to Her Majesty in right of Canada under this Act as a result of an erroneous payment, overpayment or a penalty imposed under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to an application for an income support payment made under section 5, makes a representation that they know to be false or misleading;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: being required under this Act to provide information, provides information or makes a representation that the person knows to be false or misleading;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly fails to declare to the Minister all or some of the personâs income for the period in respect of which the person applied for the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: makes an application or declaration under this Act that the person knows is false or misleading because of the non-disclosure of facts;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly, in any manner, receives an income support payment that the person is not eligible to receive under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: participates in, assents to or acquiesces in an act or omission mentioned in paragraphs (a) to (e).

Subsection.Text: No prosecution for an offence under this section may be instituted if a penalty for that conduct has been imposed under section 12.1.
Every person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) is liable on summary conviction to, as the case may be,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a fine of not more than $5,000, plus an amount of not more than double the amount of the income support payment that was or would have been paid as a result of committing the offence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: both the fine and imprisonment for a term of not more than six months.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes a provision of this Act or of the regulations is guilty of an offence.

Every person who delays or obstructs an investigator designated under subsection 14.3(1) or under an authorization issued under subsection 14.3(2) in the investigatorâs exercise of the Ministerâs powers or in their performance of the Ministerâs duties and functions under this Act is guilty of an offence.

Every person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) or (2) for which no penalty is provided is liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than $2,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both.
The Minister may designate as an investigator any person or class of persons for the purpose of enforcing sections 14.1 and 14.2.

The Minister may authorize the Commissioner, as defined in section 2 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, to designate as an investigator any employee or class of employees of the Agency, as defined in that section 2, for the purpose of enforcing sections 14.1 and 14.2.

An information or complaint about an offence under this Act may be heard, tried or determined by any provincial court judge, as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code, if the accused is resident, carrying on business, found, apprehended or in custody in the judgeâs territorial jurisdiction regardless of whether or not the matter of the information or complaint arose in that jurisdiction.

Proceedings in respect of an offence under this Act may be commenced at any time within, but not later than, five years after the day on which the Minister became aware of the subject matter of the prosecution.

Section.Text: Section 8.1 of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act applies regardless of the date on which the decision was made.
Sections 12.1 and 12.4 of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act apply regardless of the date on which the act or omission occurred.
Section 12.6 of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act applies regardless of the date on which the debt arose.
Section 7 and subsections 8(1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on March 25, 2020.

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la faillite et lâinsolvabilitÃ©

Provision.Text: subsections 50.4(2), (6), (8) and (9), section 51, subsection 66.12(5), section 66.15, subsection 66.31(1), section 102 and subsection 170.1(3) of the Act

Provision.Text: paragraph 6(b), subsections 6.1(1), 8(1), 23(1) and (2), 29(1) and (5), 41(1), (3) and (4), 42(1) and (1.2) and 43(1), sections 46, 57, 59.6 and 59.7, subsections 62(1) and (2) and 69(2), sections 71, 72 and 92 and subsection 93(1) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: RÃ¨glement sur la pension de retraite des Forces canadiennes
RÃ¨glement sur le rÃ©gime de pension de la force de rÃ©serve
Loi sur les arrangements avec les crÃ©anciers des compagnies

Provision.Text: section 11.001 and subsections 11.02(1) and 11.2(5) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la pension spÃ©ciale du service diplomatique

Provision.Text: section 7 and subsection 11(1) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la taxe dâaccise

Provision.Text: subsections 298(1) and (2), 303(7) and 304(5) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les armes Ã feu

Provision.Text: section 64 of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Garnishment, Attachment and Pension Diversion Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la saisie-arrÃªt et la distraction de pensions

Provision.Text: subsection 39(2) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi de lâimpÃ´t sur le revenu

Provision.Text: subsection 37(11), paragraph (m) of the definition investment tax credit in subsection 127(9) and subsections 152(3.1) and (4), 166.1(7) and 166.2(5) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur Investissement Canada

Provision.Text: sections 25.2 and 25.3 of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: RÃ¨glement sur les investissements susceptibles de porter atteinte Ã la sÃ©curitÃ© nationale (examen)
Loi sur la pension de retraite des lieutenants-gouverneurs

Provision.Text: subsections 3(3) and 5(1) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Members of Parliament Retiring Allowances Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les allocations de retraite des parlementaires

Provision.Text: subsections 10(1) and 32(1) of the Act

Loi sur le partage des prestations de retraite

Provision.Text: subsection 6(1) of the Act

Provision.Text: sections 6 and 9, subsections 10(4) and (5) and 25(7), section 39, subsection 40(11), section 51 and subsection 57(2) of the Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Counting of Service by Former Members of the Senate or House of Commons Regulations
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: RÃ¨glement du calcul du service des anciens membres du SÃ©nat ou de la Chambre des communes

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: RÃ¨glement gÃ©nÃ©ral sur la cession de secteurs de la fonction publique
RÃ¨glement sur la pension de la fonction publique

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Public Service Superannuation Special Election Regulations
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: RÃ¨glement sur lâexercice dâun choix spÃ©cial aux fins de la pension de la fonction publique
RÃ¨glement sur les prestations supplÃ©mentaires de dÃ©cÃ¨s

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la pension de retraite la Gendarmerie royale du Canada

Provision.Text: sections 6, 6.1 and 8, subsections 9(4) and 18(4) and section 24 of the Act
